items.title: Scheidsrechters herinneren Feyenoord en Go Ahead aan valse EL-start
items.summary: De UEFA heeft de scheidsrechtersaanstellingen voor de laatste speelronde van de competitiefase in de Europa League bekendgemaakt. Feyenoord en Go Ahead Eagles hebben een scheidsrechter die ze ook al tegenkwamen bij hun openingsduel, waarin beide clubs een valse start kenden.

items.title: Prioriteiten bij transfers Ajax zorgen voor vraagtekens: Resink nog een optie?
items.summary: Met redacteur Jos Boesveld en Matthijs Vegter worden in deze aflevering van VI ZSM de transferperikelen bij Ajax besproken. Waar ligt nu de prioriteit in Amsterdam?

items.title: As 'managers' disappear from the Premier League, are clubs really better for it?
items.summary: Premier League clubs are corporations that have outgrown the "manager" role that legendary Arsenal and Man United clubs once had. Why?

items.title: Ex-FIFA prez backs call to 'stay away' from WC
items.summary: Former FIFA president Sepp Blatter on Monday backed a proposed fan boycott of World Cup matches in the United States because of the conduct of President Donald Trump and his administration at home and abroad.

items.title: Source: USMNT's Freeman set for Villarreal move
items.summary: LaLiga side Villarreal is finalizing the acquisition of United States and Orlando City defender Alex Freeman, a source with knowledge of the deal told ESPN.

items.title: Carragher warns Slot: No UCL, no 'leg to stand on'
items.summary: Former Liverpool defender Jamie Carragher believes head coach Arne Slot would not have "a leg to stand on" if they do not qualify for the Champions League.

items.title: PSG complete signing of Barça teen Fernández
items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain have completed the signing of Barcelona's talented teenager Dro Fernández after exercising his €8 million ($9.5 million) release clause for the LaLiga side.

items.title: Waarom er dinsdag geen Champions League-wedstrijden zijn
items.summary: Eind januari staat de laatste speelronde van de Champions League op het programma. Op diverse vlakken is het nog volop spannend. In tegenstelling tot de andere speelrondes wordt er niet over twee dagen, maar over één dag gespeeld. Voetbal International legt uit waarom.

items.title: Removing US as World Cup host would be eminently sad – and entirely justified | Alexander Abnos
items.summary: A country where safety is under threat from federal violence on the streets is not fit to stage soccer’s showpiece event Removing the United States as co-host of the 2026 World Cup would hurt for pretty much everyone. Fans would miss out on seeing the sport’s pinnacle in their home towns (or somewhere nearby). Cities and businesses small and large would lose the financial benefits they had banked on. It would be a logistical and political nightmare on an international scale, the likes of which have never been seen before in sports. It would be eminently sad. And it would be entirely justified. It brings me no pleasure to say this. The United States has been eager to host a men’s World Cup for more than a decade and a half. The desire survived and even grew after 2010’s failure to out-bid Russia and Qatar (in public and behind closed doors) for the 2018 and 2022 World Cups. With hosting rights for 2026 later secured alongside Canada and Mexico, the US soccer scene prepared to show off that the sport is now part of the nation’s fabric, 32 years after hosting the tournament for the first time in 1994. Soccer’s growing popularity in America has helped inspire other US sports to try new formats, encouraged us to engage more fully with the world in a sporting context, and has been at the center of conversations about our society and culture. The 2026 World Cup was seen as the best chance for the world to fully experience not just how much the US has improved at soccer, but how much soccer has improved the US. Continue reading...

items.title: De no-nonsense aanpak van Leeroy Echteld: 'Ik zie hardheid juist als iets positiefs'
items.summary: Na het ontslag van Maarten Martens vertrouwt de ­clubleiding van AZ op de no-nonsense aanpak van Leeroy Echteld. De nieuwe hoofdtrainer weet wat hij wil, in de zoektocht naar ­stabiliteit in Alkmaar. ‘Als hij niet tevreden is, zal je dat meteen van hem horen, recht in je gezicht.’

items.title: Heracles Almelo heeft Australische centrumverdediger in beeld
items.summary: De winterse transfermarkt is geopend. In dit blog vind je alle speculaties en bevestigde transfers in Nederland. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in het buitenland.

items.title: 'It's a dream' - champions of Africa facing Arsenal
items.summary: Moroccan champions AS FAR have been building towards their moment in Fifa's Champions Cup against Arsenal on Wednesday.

items.title: Football transfer rumours: Bruno Fernandes to consider Saudi move … again?
items.summary: Today’s rumours are wet It’s been a quiet transfer window, all things considered, with even the worst internet attention-seekers refusing to don their yellow ties and take a day off school for its final day, their mum’s toy spaceship left idling in a shoebox under the bed. But there might yet be some action – not like that, how dare you – so let’s dive in. Crystal Palace are enduring a miserable season, rapidly slipping down the table and now in danger of relegation, the perfect example of how to ruin unexpected success. On the other hand, Steve Parish’s quiff still looks pristine, so swings and roundabouts, but he’s now faced with a problem: does he stop lovingly tending it to consider Nottingham Forest’s £35m bid for Jean-Philippe Mateta, or simply pretend that no such thing ever happened? Continue reading...

items.title: ‘No reason to rush’: Harry Kane in talks with Bayern over contract extension
items.summary: England captain’s deal runs to end of next season Kane and his family are settled in Munich, CEO says Bayern Munich have confirmed they are in talks to extend Harry Kane’s contract. The England captain joined from Tottenham in 2023 on a deal to the end of next season and secured a long-awaited first major trophy when Bayern won the Bundesliga last May. He has been the Bundesliga’s top scorer twice and, with 21 goals in 19 Bundesliga games this season, could chase down Robert Lewandowski’s single-season record of 41 goals. Continue reading...

items.title: Aston Villa in talks to re-sign Luiz
items.summary: Aston Villa are in talks to re-sign midfielder Douglas Luiz 18 months after he left the club for Juventus.

items.title: Duckett, Ahmed out as England bat vs Sri Lanka in deciding third ODI LIVE!
Barca president unhappy at teen's 'unpleasant' PSG move
items.summary: PSG sign 18-year-old Barcelona midfielder Dro Fernandez, with the Spanish side's president calling the situation around the transfer "unpleasant".

items.title: Champions League permutations: who needs what from final night of fixtures?
items.summary: A staggering 32 of 36 teams go into the final set of matches with their hopes of staying in the competition still alive With seven wins from seven, Arsenal have a perfect record in the league phase. Only Bayern Munich and Inter have found the net against Mikel Arteta’s team, who dismissed Atlético Madrid 4-0 in October. The bottom side, Kairat, visit the Emirates Stadium on the final night, with Arsenal needing a draw to confirm top spot and, theoretically, the most favourable last-16 draw. Continue reading...

items.title: Aiyawatt Srivaddhanaprabha: ‘It’s love and pain. Leicester is like my son, so I have to do it right’
items.summary: The Leicester City chair plays down talk of another relegation but knows the mood among fans is fraught Leicester City are hurting but Aiyawatt “Top” Srivaddhanaprabha, looking towards the pitch at the King Power Stadium, insists he shares supporters’ frustrations. He acknowledges the warm glow of their extraordinary Premier League title win almost a decade ago has long faded. He watches every game, which sometimes means tuning in from Thailand in the early hours. An 8pm kick-off in England is a 3am start in Bangkok. “I want to see the real passion of the players and the performance,” the chair says. “When it is not there, I can’t sleep, so it’s love and pain. Leicester is like my son. So I have to do it right. Of course, a son can be naughty, a son can fail the exam, a pain in your head. The son can be top of the class, graduate, have a bad girlfriend or good wife, you never know. So I feel the same, but the love is there. The responsibility is there. The first thing for me is to identify the problem and fix it.” Continue reading...

items.title: 'Worrying' PSR, Rudkin's role and never walking away - Leicester owner speaks out
items.summary: Leicester City’s owner has told Sky Sports News that, as well as a new manager, he is looking for a chief executive, commercial director and sporting director, as part of a major overhaul of the club’s structure.
